skill.name: wiki-page-writer
skill.description: Generates rich technical documentation pages with dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, source code citations, and first-principles depth. Use when writing documentation, generating wiki pages, creating technical deep-dives, or documenting specific components or systems.
skill.summary: Creates source-backed technical wiki pages with Mermaid diagrams and structured Markdown.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, Mermaid syntax, color values, and documentation text. No command execution, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection was found in SKILL.md. The only real concern is expected repository file inspection for source-backed documentation.

low_findings.title: Repository File Inspection Required
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read relevant implementation files and verify file paths to create source-backed documentation. This is expected behavior for a documentation skill, but users should run it only in repositories they intend to expose to the assistant.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require reading relevant files and citing source paths. This confirms filesystem access intent, but it supports the documented purpose and does not show malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static external command detections occur in Markdown frontmatter examples, Mermaid diagram requirements, VitePress compatibility guidance, and formatting text. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, subprocess invocation, or user input execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is inside Markdown documentation guidance and inline code examples. There is no executable script or instruction to run a command.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to descriptive frontmatter and a one-line page description example. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic library, or credential handling appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations contain prose and frontmatter fields, not cryptographic code. The file has no implementation that could perform weak hashing or encryption.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign Documentation Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections map to documentation language about systems, path validation, and Mermaid blocks. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, credential discovery, or persistence behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines describe documentation validation and diagram formatting. They do not request collection of system information outside the target codebase.

content.value_statement: Technical teams need accurate wiki pages that cite real source code. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create structured documentation with Mermaid diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Generates Markdown wiki pages for components, systems, and features.
Requires source-backed claims with file paths and line citations.
Adds at least two Mermaid diagrams using dark-mode styling guidance.
Structures pages around overview, architecture, components, data flow, implementation, and references.
Includes VitePress-compatible frontmatter and formatting constraints.
Uses tables and pseudocode to explain APIs, configs, and complex code paths.
limitations: Requires access to the target repository files for accurate citations.
Does not publish pages to a wiki or documentation site by itself.
Cannot guarantee Mermaid rendering without checking the target documentation toolchain.
May be excessive for short notes or simple README updates.

use_cases.title: Document a Service Architecture
use_cases.description: Create a source-cited wiki page that explains why a service exists, how it is structured, and how data moves through it.

use_cases.title: Prepare Onboarding Material
use_cases.description: Turn a complex code area into a readable technical guide with diagrams, tables, and verified references.

use_cases.title: Build VitePress Wiki Content
use_cases.description: Draft a VitePress-ready page with frontmatter, Markdown sections, dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, and source citations.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a wiki page for the component in [path]. Read the implementation first, cite source files, and include Mermaid diagrams.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Feature Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for behavior and data flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create technical documentation for [feature]. Cover the purpose, main code paths, data flow, configuration, and edge cases with citations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a VitePress Deep Dive
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a publishable documentation page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a VitePress wiki page for [system]. Include frontmatter, architecture sections, two dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, tables, and references.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Expand Existing Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for improving current documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing documentation for [area], compare it with the implementation, then rewrite it with verified claims and missing diagrams.

output_examples.input: Document the authentication module for the internal wiki.
output_examples.output: A Markdown wiki page with frontmatter, overview, architecture, component table, sequence diagram, data-flow diagram, implementation notes, and source references.

output_examples.input: Explain how the job queue processes tasks.
output_examples.output: A technical deep dive that traces queue entry points, worker behavior, retry handling, configuration, and failure paths with cited source locations.

output_examples.input: Create VitePress documentation for the billing service.
output_examples.output: A VitePress-compatible page with dark-mode Mermaid diagrams, API and config tables, pseudocode, and a references section.

best_practices: Run the skill from the repository root so file paths and citations can be verified.
Give a clear scope, audience, and expected page depth before generation starts.
Review Mermaid diagrams in the target documentation renderer before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to document code that the assistant cannot read.
Do not accept uncited architectural claims in the final page.
Do not use it for brief release notes or lightweight status updates.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured Markdown wiki pages for technical systems, components, and features.

faq.question: Does it require source code access?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to read relevant files so claims can include source citations.

faq.question: Can it create Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires at least two Mermaid diagrams and includes dark-mode styling guidance.

faq.question: Is it specific to VitePress?
faq.answer: It includes VitePress frontmatter and compatibility rules, but the Markdown can be adapted for other wiki tools.

faq.question: Does it publish documentation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It drafts the page content only. Publishing remains a separate workflow.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing?
faq.answer: Check source citations, Mermaid rendering, inferred claims, and whether sensitive internal details should be removed.
